scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Source of truth: the live server-rendered Picqer API reference pages (picqer.com/en/api/<resource>, fetched 2026-07-03) enumerating every resource category in the docs sidebar (customers, orders, products, picklists, warehouses, suppliers, tags, purchaseorders, returns, backorders, comments, locations, location_types, picking-containers, picklist batches, shipments, packagings, packingstations, receipts, stockhistory, users, productfields, customerfields, orderfields, pricelists, shippingproviders, vatgroups, webshoporders, hooks/webhooks). Every endpoint below is covered_by a stream/write action XOR excluded with a real category and reason: 24 GET list endpoints are implemented as streams (the original 6 legacy-parity streams plus 18 new resources), and 26 practical mutations as write actions. Excluded: single-object detail GETs whose record shape is already covered by the identically-shaped list stream (duplicate_of); nested sub-resource collections reachable only by iterating a parent id with no independent top-level list endpoint of their own, e.g. a specific order's tags/comments/backorders (out_of_scope, narrower than the top-level equivalent); binary/PDF generation (binary_payload); narrow per-record sub-object management (product images/locations/parts, customer/product tag-link toggles, warehouse-level product activation) that is a fine-grained edit of an already-covered parent record rather than an independent business object (out_of_scope); deprecated legacy fields (binary_payload/deprecated); and read-only reference/reporting data with no create/update semantics (non_data_endpoint).
